title: Microcerasus incana
taxonomy: Microcerasus incana is a species of shrub in the rose family, Rosaceae. It is a member of the genus Microcerasus, which is sometimes considered a synonym of Potentilla or a distinct genus within the Potentillinae subfamily. Microcerasus incana is native to Europe, particularly in the mountainous regions. The classification of Microcerasus incana can be summarized as follows: family Rosaceae, genus Microcerasus (or Potentilla), and species incana. Its classification is subject to ongoing research and revision in the field of botany.

intro: Microcerasus incana, also known as the blackthorn or sloe, is a thorny shrub native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Microcerasus incana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make jams, wines, and teas, as well as a liqueur, sloe gin, which is made by infusing the berries in gin.
